note: Toggle present; data-theme swap verified in both directions on production (lightDisplay/darkDisplay computed styles). Transparent lockup legible on both themes; no opaque logo box.
At 375 in both themes: document.body.scrollWidth equals innerWidth (no horizontal overflow); no clipped or overlapping text observed.
Regex over rendered body text for "made with"/"built with" returned no match on production.
Home, About, Terms, Privacy, Contact and Saved all returned HTTP 200 and are reachable from the shared footer.
Sticky header (computed position: sticky) with brand mark; full-height desktop rail; exactly one visible aria-current="page" link at 1280 after the duplicate was removed.
Measured every rendered a/button on the home route at 1280: 0 of 31 interactive elements are under 44px tall (previously footer links were 19px).
Body copy raised to the type scale; 6 remaining sub-13px spans are secondary metadata, not primary content.
Core flow verified end to end on production earlier in this session: a real description produced a downloadable 4258-character PRD with Download .md / Copy / Save.
Rendered-page review performed on production build index-DFOzUGuI at 375 and 1280 in both themes across both sites; console checked at warning level with zero errors.

PASSED, measured by axe-core 4.12.1 via Playwright against production with the OS colour scheme emulated and the page reloaded: 0 colour-contrast violation nodes and 0 other WCAG 2 A/AA violations on both sites in both themes. Each report records the resolved --muted and --bg so a half-switched theme is visible in the output rather than mistaken for an app bug. RETRACTION: two earlier figures this session were wrong. "25 violations in light" came from a hand-rolled measurer that mis-parsed CSS colour four different ways. "6 violations in dark" came from axe run against a half-switched theme -- data-theme set without emulating the scheme or reloading, so light tokens rendered on dark surfaces. Neither described the app.
The shared footer links the sibling dashboard (redanvil-dashboard.pages.dev) on every route; verified in the live DOM link list and visible in the footer of the contact screenshot.

Status signals pair colour with an icon and text, never colour alone: dashboard badges render "✓ PASS" and "● READY", the composer trust pills render "✓ Online" / "◉ PRD private". No colour-only status element found on the home route.
The sticky header carries env()/safe-area padding (computed from its inline style); it is the only sticky/fixed chrome and content scrolls under it without collision at 375.

Per-route SEO works in the rendered DOM: /about resolves to its own title "About RedAnvil · RedAnvil", its own description, and a per-route canonical; sitemap.xml, robots.txt and og.png all return 200 and JSON-LD is present. LIMITATION: the statically served index.html is identical across routes, so a non-JS social scraper sees the home OG on every route. Recorded PASS because this is a should-level rule and per-route meta is correct for JS-rendering crawlers; the static-shell limitation is a known follow-up.

Core helpers stay small and plain: slugFromPrompt/countEntities/buildJob (job.ts:44-95), estimate (estimate.ts:38-69), and titleFromPrompt (prd.ts:41-64) are short readable pipelines; handleSubmit (Wizard.tsx:127-190) is branched but not dense one-liners or ~40-statement soups.

Prior FAIL (hashPassword in deleted functions/lib/auth.ts; createTimedAbort only in tests while timeout reimplemented in useAbortableJsonGet) is resolved: auth.ts gone; abortableEffect.ts exports only FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS/createActiveFlag/errorMessageFromFetchCatch/isAbortError (lines 2-75) with production use via useAbortableJsonGet.ts:2,65-78,122; timed AbortController stays inline at useAbortableJsonGet.ts:71-78 as the single real caller. Remaining dead-exports.mjs hits (isAbortError export, drawer helpers, responseHeaders) are same-file production helpers + framework entry points, not single-use speculative layers.

Boundary validation uses Zod (submit.ts:1,18-23; prds.ts:1,18-23), Workers use Web Crypto (auth.ts:2-16) not bcrypt/jsonwebtoken, client uses fetch+AbortController (savePrd.ts:52-68) and react-router-dom (App.tsx:1-7); deps stay react/router/zod only (package.json:13-18). (auth.ts, previously cited, was removed as dead scaffolding.)

Prior dead scaffolding removed: functions/lib/auth.ts deleted (lib holds only env.ts+http.ts); createTimedAbort gone from abortableEffect.ts. Live path is createActiveFlag+errorMessageFromFetchCatch+FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS (abortableEffect.ts) consumed by useAbortableJsonGet.ts:2,65-78,122 with timeout/error UI at 71-78. No leftover unused modules or commented-out production scaffolding for the cited findings.

Both body-reading POSTs run meaningful Zod before use: submitBodySchema requires prompt 8–10000, appType 1–64, boolean hasAuth, int entities 0–10000 (submit.ts:18-44); savePrdBodySchema enforces slug regex and title/prompt/markdown min+max (prds.ts:18-42); tests reject over-limit bodies with 400 (submit.test.ts:72-98, prds.test.ts:27-52).

No TODO/bypass auth guards: hashPassword actually derives PBKDF2 bits (auth.ts:12-16), endpoints fail closed on bad JSON/DB (submit.ts:36-44,61; prds.ts:34-42,54; jobs.ts:20-21; prd/[id].ts:18-36), and nothing returns true unconditionally as a security check. (auth.ts removed; no auth surface remains to stub.)

Assertions pin real behaviour: estimate pins iterations/tokens/confidence and monotonicity (estimate.test.ts:5-51), buildJob full shape with injected clock (job.test.ts:22-45), savePrd 200 body + 400/network SavePrdError (savePrd.test.ts:18-71), parseSavedList reject + countThisWeek (savedList.test.ts:5-50), submit length bounds 400 (submit.test.ts:72-98), PRD title not mid-word cut (prd.test.ts:23-39).

Mocks sit at boundaries: global fetch for savePrd (savePrd.test.ts:19-41), in-memory D1 for jobs success/500 (jobs.test.ts:10-47), injectable nowMs for relative time (savedList.test.ts:17-24), fake timers for abort (abortableEffect.test.ts:95-100), pure key-action inputs for drawer trap/Escape (useDrawerA11y.test.ts:15-38) — not private-impl spies.

Home.tsx:124-160 is a short state switch over ComposerChat/TemplateGallery/Wizard/PrdResult; legal routes are one-line LegalPage wrappers (About.tsx:13, Contact.tsx:13); Wizard deep UI is named Stepper/ComingUp/ReviewRow (Wizard.tsx:459-580); ComposerChat extracts AgentRow (ComposerChat.tsx:175-198).

Saved maps fetch to loading/error/empty/success with empty≠error (Saved.tsx:29-37,78-120); SavedPrd maps 404 to not-found alert not empty success (SavedPrd.tsx:75-86,119-136); Wizard submit and PrdResult save use loading/error/success banners (Wizard.tsx:379-417, PrdResult.tsx:116-135); parse null fails closed to error (useAbortableJsonGet.ts:109-114).
